output: Added the three DB-snapshot listeners to `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py`: `on_raw_reaction_clear`, `on_raw_reaction_clear_emoji`, and `on_raw_message_delete`. Each path enumerates `get_active_reactors(...)`, writes one moderation-decision row per active reactor with the correct classification, and keeps `is_suspicious=False`. `on_raw_message_delete` reads `get_message_snapshot(payload.message_id)` at handler entry before any await, so it stays ahead of LoggerCog’s soft-delete path and falls back to null author/content fields when the snapshot is unavailable. Verified the module with `python -m py_compile src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` and wrote the batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/design-and-implement-a-20260419-1105/execution_batch_5.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `on_raw_reaction_clear`, `on_raw_reaction_clear_emoji`, and `on_raw_message_delete` listeners to `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py`. Verified each path enumerates `db_handler.get_active_reactors(...)`, writes one moderation-decision row per active reactor with the requested classification, and sets `is_suspicious=False`; `on_raw_message_delete` reads `get_message_snapshot(payload.message_id)` at the top of the handler before any await, then uses DB snapshots only with graceful null fallbacks when snapshot data is missing.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed the three new listeners are registered in `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py`. Each one uses `get_active_reactors` and writes one row per reactor with `classification` set to `moderator_cleared_all`, `moderator_cleared_emoji`, or `message_deleted_cascade` respectively, with `is_suspicious=False` on all three paths. `on_raw_message_delete` reads the message snapshot synchronously at handler entry before any await, so it stays ahead of LoggerCog's soft-delete race; when `get_active_reactors` returns an empty list, the handlers no-op.
